q: What is the PEEL paragraph structure?
What does the CRAAP test evaluate?
Which referencing style is most common in UK universities?
What is the main difference between quantitative and qualitative research?
What is Gibbs' Reflective Cycle used for?
opts: Reflecting on experiences to learn from them

q: What is an ad hominem fallacy?
opts: Attacking the person instead of the argument

q: What does informed consent mean in research?
opts: Participants are paid for their time

q: What is the Pomodoro Technique?
opts: Working in focused 25-minute blocks with breaks

q: What is a thesis statement?
opts: The main argument of an essay
The title of a research paper

q: What is the difference between a premise and a conclusion?
opts: Premises are reasons given to support a conclusion
Conclusions are reasons given to support premises

q: What is a straw man fallacy?
opts: Misrepresenting an argument to make it easier to attack
Using a weak argument to support a strong claim
Attacking the person making the argument

q: What is the purpose of a literature review?
opts: To summarise existing research on a topic

q: What does SMART stand for in goal setting?
What is the SQ3R reading method?
What is the difference between confidentiality and anonymity in research?
opts: Confidentiality means not sharing data; anonymity means not identifying participants
Anonymity means not sharing data; confidentiality means not identifying participants
Both mean participants' data is deleted after the study

q: What is a false dichotomy?
opts: Presenting only two options when more exist
A false statement about two things

q: What is the purpose of a reference list?
opts: To list all sources cited in your work
To show everything you have read
To acknowledge sources you didn't use

q: What is the difference between primary and secondary research?
opts: Primary is original data collection; secondary analyses existing data
Primary is more important than secondary
Primary uses numbers; secondary uses words
Primary is done first; secondary is done later

q: What is the Eisenhower Matrix used for?
opts: Prioritising tasks by urgency and importance

q: What is the purpose of a conclusion in an essay?
opts: To synthesise arguments and discuss implications
